content.full_text:
f'

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 9, 1993

Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor
591 Redwood Highway #4000
Mill Valley, Ca 94941

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-386

Dear Mr. Nielsen:

You have requested advice on behalf of the Governor Pete
Wilson Committee (the "Committee") under the campaign reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

May the Committee send a mass mailing (either an invitation
to a fundraising event or a campaign communication) in an envelope
which identifies the sender as the Committee and uses a local
street address which is one of several local street addresses
contained on an attachment to the Committee's Statement of
Organization?

CONCLUSION

The Committee may send out mass mailings which identify the
Committee as the sender and indicate a local street address which
is one of multiple addresses on record with the Secretary of State
as part of the Committee's Statement of Organization.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Nielsen, Vigo
Our File No. A-93-386
Page No. 2


FACTS

You are writing on behalf of the Committee which is raising
funds and conducting a statewide campaign to support Pete Wilson's
reelection in November 1994. It filed its Statement of
Organization (Form 410) on April 26, 1993, identifying its
headquarters street address in Sacramento. The Committee is
conducting the campaign and fundraising out of other offices
throughout the state, whose addresses were listed on an attachment
to the Form 410. The list of addresses was amended on July 7,
1993 and August 20, 1993.

On September 2, 1993, the Secretary of State informed you
that amendments adding supplemental addresses would not be filed.
That letter explained that a committee may not have more than one
address for public disclosure purposes and that adding additional
addresses was extraneous to the Statement of Organization.

ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 84102 states:

The statement of organization required by Section 84101
shall include:

(a) The name, street address and telephone
number, if any, of the committee....

The Act does not specifically limit committees to a single
street address.

Government Code Section 84305 states:

(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), no
candidate or committee shall send a mass mailing
unless the name, street address, and city of the
candidate or committee are shown on the outside of
each piece of mail in the mailing.... A post office
box may be stated in lieu of a street address if the
organization's address is a matter of public record
with the Secretary of State.

Since the Act clearly allows the use of a post office box in
lieu of a committee's street address to identify it as the sender
of a mass mailing, allowing the use of another street address is
not counter to the intent of the Act. Although the committee's
post office box is not required to be listed on the statement of
organization, inclusion of any other street address the committee
intends to use for mass mailings would be consistent with the
language of Sections 84102 and 84305.

Nielsen, Vigo
Our File No. A-93-386
Page No. 3


We appreciate the inclusion of all relevant facts as well as
your analysis of the applicable law in your letter.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

cc: Secretary of State
